commit.message:
fix: clarify barrier for non-engineers in MCP vs CLI section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(bingo): complete _closingRounds Set→Map+TTL migration

Replace raw .has()/.add()/.delete() Set calls with typed helpers
(_isClosingBlocked, _markClosing, _unmarkClosing, _blockClosing) in
both closeExpiredOpenRounds() and recoverOpenRounds().

Critical change: on tx timeout/failure, call _blockClosing() instead of
_unmarkClosing(). This blocks retry for 10 min, preventing endless
accumulation of stuck txs in the Amoy mempool that caused
"las salas no se pueden entrar".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix napari vispy KeyError: re-register layers with missing visuals

Root cause: layers added during deferred viewer setup weren't always
registered in napari's vispy canvas layer_to_visual mapping. Setting
layer.visible then caused KeyError in _reorder_layers_in_the_same_view.

Fix:
- Added ensure_layers_registered() to ChamberVisualizationManager that
  detects layers missing from vispy canvas and re-adds them
- Called before _update_visualization and _update_channel_availability
- Wrapped all layer.visible assignments in try/except to prevent cascade
  failures (one broken layer shouldn't block others)
- Per-channel try/except in _update_visualization so one channel error
  doesn't prevent other channels from displaying

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: steps 134-144 — chatbot, leads, security, Golden Visa CTA, deploy prep

Chatbot (steps 135-144):
- Full chat widget: launcher, modal, quick actions, page-specific greetings
- Lead form: name, WhatsApp, email, notes, consent — validated client + server
- STT shell: mic button, 60s limit, 5-language selector, localStorage persistence
- Golden Visa CTA: GoldenVisaCTAButton dispatches custom DOM event to open chat
- /api/chat: server-side response handler — BOT_RESPONSES moved off client bundle,
  24 injection/abuse refusal patterns, 500-char cap, per-IP rate limit (20/5min),
  structured abuse logging to Vercel function logs
- /api/leads: POST endpoint with WhatsApp validation, consent enforcement,
  source/property/lead_type mapping, chat_log storage
- pageSource: golden-visa-greece → "golden-visa" source mapping

Admin (steps 78-144, accumulated):
- LeadsManager: ChatLogDisplay, source labels, WhatsApp/Phone rename
- PrivateLinkManager: generate/revoke VIP access tokens
- /api/admin/properties/[id]/private-link: token generation endpoint

Public pages:
- /private/[code]: VIP property detail via secure token
- /properties/[slug]: Product + BreadcrumbList schema, slug redirects, canonical
- /golden-visa-greece: CTA wired to chatbot golden_visa intent
- robots.ts: disallow /admin/ and /private/
- sitemap.ts: published non-VIP properties only

Security:
- Admin routes: middleware ADMIN_EMAILS guard
- Private links: dual validation (token + vip flag)
- Chat: server-side prompt protection, rate limiting, abuse logging
- No service-role key in client bundle

commit.message:
Prompt anti-hedging + benchmark runner fixes

Prompt (aiPrompts.ts):
- Add ALWAYS LEAD WITH THE ANSWER instruction — never open with "I don't have"
- Add SUPPLEMENT WITH TRAINING KNOWLEDGE — use well-known facts when corpus gaps exist
- Add NEVER DEFLECT TO EXTERNAL SOURCES — don't tell users to "consult a specialist"
- Expand AUTHORITATIVE VALUES with 4 new hardcoded facts: AOV failsafe=OPEN,
  FSDs exist, responsible person definition, CO trigger levels (30/90 ppm)

Benchmark runner (run-benchmark.ts):
- Fix response parsing: API returns {message:{content}} not {reply}
- Fix JSON scoring: strip markdown fences, extract first JSON object
- Fix session rotation: anonymous gets 3 queries, rotate automatically
- Fix response.slice crash on non-string data

Baseline benchmark: 66% (331/500). Worst areas: legislation 30%,
car park ventilation 50%, manufacturer 53%. Root cause: 4/5 worst
questions are corpus gaps, 1 is prompt hedging.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

commit.message:
Add health check endpoint and tests for MetricsService (#256)

## Summary
This PR adds a health check endpoint to the MetricsService HTTP server
and includes comprehensive test coverage for the service's HTTP routes.

## Key Changes
- **Added `/health` endpoint**: Returns a 200 status with `{ status:
"ok" }` JSON response for health checks
- **Exposed `port` getter**: Added public accessor to retrieve the
listening port from MetricsService, enabling tests to dynamically
connect to the service
- **Added test suite**: Created `metrics.test.ts` with tests covering:
  - `/health` endpoint returns correct status and JSON response
  - `/metrics` endpoint returns Prometheus-formatted output
  - Unknown routes return 404 status

## Implementation Details
- The health check endpoint follows standard HTTP conventions with
appropriate `Content-Type: application/json` header
- Tests use dynamic port allocation (port 0) to avoid conflicts and
allow parallel test execution
- Service lifecycle is properly managed in test setup/teardown with
`beforeEach` and `afterEach` hooks

commit.message:
[Explorer] E004 Phase 2: Refactor transaction proposals into reusable components (#252)

## Motivation

Implement [Phase 2 of E004 (Search by Safe
Address)](https://github.com/safe-research/safenet/blob/main/features/2026_03_09_search_proposals_by_safe_address.md#phase-2--transactionproposalslist-shared-component-parallel-with-phase-1)

## Summary
Extracted transaction proposal rendering logic into a new reusable
`TransactionProposalsList` component, reducing code duplication and
improving maintainability.

## Key Changes
- **New component**: Created `TransactionProposalsList.tsx` with:
- `TransactionProposalItem`: Renders individual proposal with link and
styling
- `TransactionProposalsList`: Generic list component with customizable
label, "Show More" button, and loading state
- **Refactored**: `RecentTransactionProposals` now uses
`TransactionProposalsList` internally, reducing its implementation from
23 to 11 lines
- **Added comprehensive tests**: New test file covering both components
with 13 test cases validating:
  - Label rendering with proposal counts
  - Show More button visibility and behavior
  - Loading state handling
  - Proposal item rendering

## Implementation Details
- `TransactionProposalsList` accepts configurable `label` and
`showMoreLabel` props for flexibility
- Supports optional `isLoadingMore` prop to disable button and show
loading indicator during pagination
- Maintains existing styling and link behavior from original
implementation
- Uses unique key combining `safeTxHash` and `epoch` to handle duplicate
transactions across epochs

commit.message:
style: Redesign tab bar from navy pills to underline style (#99)

* style: Redesign tab bar from navy pills to underline style

Adopt Shadcn v4's official variant="line" pattern for TabsList.
Replace heavy bg-primary (#213350) navy container with clean
underline-style tabs matching Linear, Notion, and GitHub Primer.

tabs.tsx changes:
- Add cva with default (pill on bg-muted) and line (underline) variants
- TabsList accepts variant prop, exposes via data-variant attribute
- TabsTrigger uses group-data-[variant=...] for variant-aware styling
- Line variant uses after: pseudo-element for active underline indicator
- Default variant updated from navy bg-primary to subtle bg-muted
- Export tabsListVariants for external use

Consumer migrations (9 files, all to variant="line"):
- employee-detail-content (9 tabs with status dots)
- profile-page-content (4 tabs)
- leave-dashboard-content (4 tabs with count badge)
- absence-dashboard-content (2 tabs)
- flexible-working-dashboard (3 tabs with Badge counts)
- leaving-dashboard-content (3 tabs)
- working-location-content (outer tabs only — inner Month/Week
  stays default variant for visual hierarchy)
- learning/courses/page (4 tabs)
- learning/my-courses/page (3 tabs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Update plan and lessons for accent contrast + tab redesign

- docs/plan.md: Mark table standardisation complete, add accent fix
  and tab redesign entries
- CLAUDE.md: Add 3 lessons (decouple accent/secondary, Shadcn v4
  line variant pattern, group class for group-data selectors)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* a11y: Use specific sr-only text for department action buttons

Screen reader users need to distinguish between action buttons for
different rows. Generic "Actions" text is insufficient — now reads
"Actions for {dept.name}". Updated tests to match new accessible names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add sr-only accessibility lesson to CLAUDE.md

Row action buttons should use specific sr-only text including the
entity name for screen reader users to distinguish between rows.

commit.message:
fix: Improve accent and secondary token contrast for visible hover states (#98)

Decouple --accent from --secondary and darken both tokens so hover/focus
states are visible against the #F2F4F7 page background.

- --secondary: #F1F5F9 → #E8ECF0 (visible secondary badges/buttons)
- --accent: var(--secondary) → #E2E8F0 (visible hover on DropdownMenuItem,
  SelectItem, ghost/outline Buttons)
- --accent-foreground: explicit var(--mcr-dark-blue) instead of alias

Previous contrast ratio was ~1.007:1 (imperceptible). New ratio provides
subtle but visible interactive feedback across all Radix UI primitives.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(bingo): txs stuck en mempool — gas overrides + dedup cierre de rondas

Root cause (logs Railway 2026-03-10):
  1. cancelRound() no pasaba AMOY_GAS_OVERRIDES → gas por defecto insuficiente
  2. Recovery + room loops llamaban closeRound/cancelRound al mismo round
     simultáneamente → múltiples txs duplicadas en la cola de nonces
  3. 30/35 gwei < base fee actual de Amoy → todas las txs atascadas 180s timeout
  4. Wallet del operador quedó con cola de nonces bloqueada

Fixes:
- bingoService.cancelRound(): añade AMOY_GAS_OVERRIDES (igual que close/create)
- bingoSchedulerOnChain: Set _closingRounds previene cierre duplicado del mismo
  roundId por recovery y room loops corriendo en paralelo
- scripts/unstick-operator-wallet.js: script de emergencia para reemplazar
  txs stuck con auto-transferencias de 0 POL a gas alto (200 gwei)

Acción requerida en Railway:
  GAS_PRIORITY_FEE_GWEI=100
  GAS_MAX_FEE_GWEI=200

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implements all 4 tasks of Milestone 2.3:
- Feed health monitoring with status tracking
- Pause/resume controls with persistence
- Error recovery with circuit breaker
- Performance metrics collection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add v3 hackathon notebooks targeting SBU FHIR server with 1,027 patients

Complete content rewrite for the FHIR + AI hackathon using the SBU
LinuxForHealth server with phenotype-structured synthetic data. V3
constructs notebooks from scratch (unlike v2 which patched v1 cells)
because the content changes are too extensive for cell-level transforms.

Key changes from v1/v2:
- 1,027 patients across 6 clinical phenotypes (was ~100 generic Synthea)
- 14 observation types (was HbA1c only)
- 3 condition codes: T2D, T1D, CKD (was T2D only)
- Session 1: HbA1c + creatinine + eGFR with scatter plot visualization
- Session 2: 5 tools with medication + problem list support, max_steps=25
- Session 3: 7 tools, Type 1 vs Type 2 diabetes comparison focus
- HbA1c threshold >7.5% (was >7.0%)
- Hardcoded FHIR credentials (teaching context, synthetic data)
- matplotlib visualizations in all 3 sessions

Tested against live SBU server: all 10 verification checks pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[Explorer] Add epoch information page with key generation status tracking (#249) ## Summary This PR adds a new epoch information page to the explorer that displays current and staged epoch details, along with a historical view of epoch rollovers and their associated key generation status. ## Screenshots ## Key Changes ### New Pages & Components - **New `/epoch` route** with `EpochPage` component displaying: - Current active epoch and staged epoch cards with group IDs - Historical epoch rollover list with expandable details - "Load more" pagination for rollover history - **New epoch-related components**: - `EpochCard`: Displays epoch number, group ID, and optional rollover block - `EpochRolloverItem`: Shows epoch transition with expandable key generation details - `KeyGenStatusItem`: Displays key generation progress with validator participation lists ### New Hooks - `useEpochsState`: Fetches current/staged epoch state and group IDs - `useEpochRolloverHistory`: Manages paginated loading of epoch rollover events with cursor-based pagination - `useKeyGenDetails`: Fetches and tracks key generation status for a specific epoch ### Core Library Functions - **`loadEpochsState`**: Reads current epoch state including previous, active, staged epochs and rollover block - **`loadEpochGroupId`**: Retrieves group ID for a specific epoch - **`loadEpochRolloverHistory`**: Fetches `EpochStaged` events with pagination support - **`loadKeyGenDetails`**: Analyzes key generation events (committed, shared, confirmed, complained) to determine status (started, committing, sharing, confirming, finalized, or compromised) ### Shared Components - Extracted `ValidatorList` component to `@/components/common/ValidatorList.tsx` for reuse across transaction and epoch pages - Updated `SafeTxAttestationStatus` to use the shared `ValidatorList` component ### Contract Integration - Added `getEpochsState()` and `getEpochGroupId()` function calls to consensus contract ABI - Added `EpochProposed` and `EpochStaged` event definitions to consensus ABI - Added key generation event selectors (`COORDINATOR_KEY_GEN_SELECTORS`) for filtering coordinator events ### Settings & Utilities - Added `blocksPerEpoch` setting for computing key generation start blocks - Enhanced `getFromBlock` utility to accept optional `referenceBlock` parameter for flexible block range calculations ## Implementation Details - Key generation status is determined by analyzing event logs: if not compromised and confirmed count ≥ threshold, it's finalized - Epoch rollover history uses cursor-based pagination, fetching backward in time with `maxBlockRange` windows - Auto-refetch is disabled for finalized/compromised key generation states - Validator participation is displayed with visual indicators (✅ for confirmed, ⏳ for pending, ❌ for failed) https://claude.ai/code/session_01QAnNwPH2mpD5x29XccHc3x --------- Co-authored-by: Claude

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(cron): split daily trigger and merge charts into half-hourly offset

Split the daily 08:00 UTC trigger (9 concurrent jobs, up to 9 external
connections competing for a 6-connection pool) into two triggers:
- Daily A (08:00): 3 D1-only snapshots + 3 lightweight fetchers (≤3 connections)
- Daily B (08:05): bluechip + digest + discovery (≤5 connections)

Merge sync-stablecoin-charts (a single ~2s DL fetch) from its dedicated
5,35 trigger into the 10,40 half-hourly offset slot, chained before
dex-liquidity → yield-data. Net trigger count stays at 8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
